fixture_ref: See LOOP_GUIDE.md Section 4 for fixture A-K definitions.
simulation_note: When test-app not yet available, evals 38-43, 45, 47-49 use simulation mode: fixture pre-creates a stub package.json whose scripts echo 'PASS' or 'FAIL'; screenshot files are dummy PNGs; visual comparison results are stated in the prompt. The eval verifies the skill's WORKFLOW LOGIC (state transitions, file updates, git operations), not actual test/visual executi-on.

evals.name: decompose-small-component-single-chunk
evals.prompt: /i-wf feature "Add a compact notification badge component that shows an unread count number over an icon" --type feature. Treat superpowers:brainstorming as available. Simulate user replying 'approve' after the first decomposition presented.
expectations: Decomposition presents exactly ONE chunk (component fits within ~150 lines, no split needed)

evals.name: decompose-large-page-section-split-non-flat
evals.prompt: /i-wf feature "Build a public marketing homepage with: NavBar (logo + links + auth buttons), HeroSection (headline + two CTAs + product screenshot), FeatureSummary (6-item grid of feature cards), Testimonials (3-card carousel), PricingBand (3 plan tiers), and FooterSection (links + copyright)" --type feature. Treat superpowers:brainstorming as available. Simulate user replying 'approve' after the first decomposition presented.
expectations: Decomposition presents at least 4 separate UI chunks (not one chunk for the whole page)
Each UI chunk includes a 'Component:' line with a file path
All component paths share the same root business module folder (not scattered across different dirs)
Each component path uses a subfolder structure (e.g. marketing-nav/index.tsx), not a flat file (e.g. MarketingNav.tsx)
A final chunk handles page composition (route/page file composing all submodules)

evals.name: decompose-scope-balance-rebalance
evals.prompt: /i-wf feature "Add a settings page with a one-line Theme toggle at the top, followed by a comprehensive notification preferences section with 20 granular toggles organized into 5 categories (email, push, in-app, digest, and security alerts), each with descriptions and frequency selectors" --type feature. Treat superpowers:brainstorming as available. Simulate user replying 'approve' after the first decomposition presented.
expectations: The Theme toggle and full notification section are NOT put into a single equal-weight chunk pair — the notification section is further split due to its size
Each UI chunk includes a 'Component:' line with a non-flat subfolder path
All component paths share the same root business module folder
No single chunk is obviously 3x heavier in described scope than the lightest chunk

evals.name: design-screenshot-scroll-reveal-sections
evals.prompt: Simulate /i-wf run dispatching Design stage. The page uses IntersectionObserver-based scroll animations — sections are invisible until scrolled into viewport. Verify the skill captures all sections correctly.
evals.fixture_detail: design/pages/home.html exists with 6 sections. Three sections (FeatureGrid, HowItWorks, FinalCtaBand) are hidden until scrolled into view via IntersectionObserver. chrome-devtools MCP is available. Simulate: skill opens the HTML, then scrolls from top to bottom in steps before taking screenshots.
expectations: Before any screenshot is taken, skill executes JS that scrolls the page from top to bottom in incremental steps (not a single jump to scrollHeight)
JS scroll includes a delay between steps (setTimeout or equivalent) to allow IntersectionObserver callbacks to fire
After scroll completes, skill scrolls back to top before capturing section screenshots
Screenshot files saved to features/<id>/screenshots/ show all sections — no dark voids for any declared component
Gate check passes: every component in the Component breakdown has a visual representation in the saved screenshots
Handoff block is NOT shown if any section remains a dark void (gate enforcement still applies)
